title: Substack vs Omnisend (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Substack vs Omnisend. See pricing, features, and which email marketing tool suits your needs best.
intro: Substack and Omnisend serve fundamentally different purposes in the email marketing landscape. Substack is built for individual writers and creators monetizing newsletters directly, while Omnisend is designed for e-commerce businesses managing multi-channel customer communication. Your choice depends entirely on whether you're building a paid publication or running an online store.

comparison_table.tool_a: Revenue-share (0% to 10% commission on subscriber payments)
comparison_table.tool_b: Freemium ($0-$16+/month based on subscribers and features)

comparison_table.tool_a: Paid subscriptions, podcast hosting, notes feed, audience recommendations
comparison_table.tool_b: Email, SMS, push notifications, omnichannel automation, product picker

choose_a_if: You're an individual writer or creator wanting to monetize your newsletter directly
You need unlimited subscribers on a completely free plan
You want built-in audience discovery and podcast hosting features
choose_b_if: You run an e-commerce business needing email and SMS together
You want advanced automation, segmentation, and multi-channel capabilities
You need reliable customer support and higher user satisfaction ratings

faq.a: Neither is universally 'better'—they serve different markets. Substack excels for individual creators monetizing content, while Omnisend is superior for e-commerce businesses managing customer communications across multiple channels. Choose based on your specific use case rather than overall superiority.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Substack or Omnisend?
faq.a: Substack is cheaper if you have unlimited free subscribers, as it charges 0% commission until you monetize. Omnisend's free tier caps at 250 subscribers, making it more expensive for growing lists. However, Omnisend may be cheaper for established e-commerce brands because you own your subscriber data and don't share revenue.

faq.q: Can I switch from Omnisend to Substack?
faq.a: Technically yes, but they're designed for different purposes. You can export your email list from Omnisend and import it to Substack, but you'll lose SMS functionality, automation workflows, and e-commerce integrations. The switch only makes sense if you're transitioning from selling products to publishing a paid newsletter as your primary business model.
